resume_summary: Built FastAPI services and reduced latency by 40 percent.
current_question: How would you handle consistency during regional failover?
candidate_answer: I would use active-active routing with bounded staleness and conflict resolution. I would define RPO and RTO and test failover quarterly.
human_feedback: Strong technical depth and clear failover intent. You should explain conflict resolution details and validation strategy. Add one concrete consistency model and tradeoff.
next_best_question: Which consistency model would you choose for cross-region writes and why?

resume_summary: Improved bundle size and page load performance.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: How did you reduce bundle size in your last project?

current_question: What tradeoffs did you make while optimizing load time?
candidate_answer: I lazy-loaded routes and removed heavy libraries. I also introduced code splitting. It improved LCP from 3.1s to 1.9s.
human_feedback: Clear and relevant answer with measurable impact. Add details on any regressions and how you monitored user impact after release.
next_best_question: How did you guard against regressions after introducing route-level code splitting?

resume_summary: Led roadmap planning across two teams.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: Tell me about a time you handled conflicting priorities.

current_question: How did you decide what to ship first?
candidate_answer: I gathered input from stakeholders and shipped what seemed most important.
human_feedback: You addressed the theme but lacked concrete decision criteria and outcomes. Use STAR and include the metric impact of your final prioritization choice.
next_best_question: What explicit criteria and metrics did you use to rank the competing requests?
